Motion And Notice Of Motion To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicole A. Aaronson, Esq., Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP, telephone 213-443-5100, appearing for Plaintiff; (CourtCall)
Kyle L. dickson, Esq., Murray Lobb, Telephone 281-488-0630, appearing for Defendant(s)clint Walston, et al:
Richard Schramm, Esq., Employment Rights Attorneys, Telephone 408-796-7551, appearing for Defendant(s)clint Walston, et al:

Ruling - ARGUED.

DEFENDANT CLINT WALSTON, SYNERGY REPROGRAPHICS, LLC Motion To Stay Proceedings

Defendants and Cross-Complainants Clint Walton and Synergy Reprographics, LLC's motion to stay this action pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 410.30(a) in favor of the earlier-filed action pending in Texas state court is denied.

A trial court considering a forum non conveniens issue engages in a two-step process. The first step is to determine whether a suitable alternative forum exists. (National Football League v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 902, 917.) Where (as here) there is a suitable alternative forum, the court proceeds to the next step, consideration of the private interests of the parties and the public interest in keeping the case in California. (Id.) The private interest factors are those that make trial and the enforceability of the ensuing judgment expeditious and relatively inexpensive, such as the ease of access to sources of proof, the cost of obtaining attendance of witnesses, and the availability of compulsory process for attendance of unwilling witnesses. (Id.) The public interest factors include avoidance of overburdening California courts, protecting potential jurors who should not be called on to decide cases in which the local community has little concern, and weighing the competing ties of California and the alternate jurisdiction to the litigation. (Id.; see also Leadford v. Leadford (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 571, 575.) The defendant, as the moving party, bears the burden of proof on a motion based on forum non conveniens. (Id. at 918.) The trial court's weighing and balancing of private and public factors is reviewed pursuant to an abuse of discretion standard. (Id.)

Here, Defendants have not met their burden to show that the pertinent factors weigh in favor of staying this action in favor of the Texas action. First, while Mr. Walston apparently is now a Texas resident, he alleges in the cross-complaint that he worked in San Francisco from 2006 to 2017 and that his damages were the result of Cross-Defendants' actions while he was located in San Francisco. Further, Synergy, which joined in the motion, apparently has its principal place of business in San Francisco and regularly conducts business in California. Moreover, the gravamen of the action relates to the termination of Mr. Walston's relationship with Plaintiff Gill Reprographics, Inc. in 2017. Many witnesses to the parties' cross-claims against each other, as well as documents and other evidence, therefore are likely to be located in California and subject to the process of this Court. Second, California has a substantial interest in enforcing its policies against misappropriation of trade secrets, unfair competition, intentional interference with contract, and tortious interference with prospective economic relations allegedly arising out of conduct occurring in California and affecting the operation of a California business and its employees. Third, the Texas action was filed only two months before this action, and Defendants did not seek a stay of this action until they filed the instant motion in January 2019, shortly before the approaching March 22, 2019 hearing on Plaintiffs' motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication and April 22, 2019 trial date. Counsel for plaintiff to submit form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312.

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Randy Hak, Esq. 213-443-5100 of wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP for Plaintiff Gill Reprographics through Court Call.
Grainne Calan, Esq. 408-796-7551 of Employment Rights Attorneys for Defendant CLINT WALSTON, SYNERGY REPROGRAPHICS, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant contests and seeks clarification. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentatvie ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CLINT WALSTON, SYNERGY REPROGRAPHICS, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Defendants Clint Walston and Synergy Reprographics, LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings on all eight causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Gill Reprographics, Inc. is denied as to all eight causes of action. Notwithstanding defendants' failure to meet and confer in "person or by telephone" as required by CCP 439, the court will address the merits of the motion. Paragraph 25 of the complaint adequately identifies Gill's claimed trade secrets that were allegedly misappropriated by defendants to withstand a pleading motion. The court does not address the distinct issue of compliance with CCP 2019.210, which is not raised by this motion. The second through eighth causes of action are not preempted by CUTSA. That statute "does not displace noncontract claims that, although related to a trade secret misappropriation, are independent and based on facts distinct from the facts that support the misappropriation claim." (Angelica Textile Servs. v. Park, (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 495, 506.) Gill's allegations that defendants diverted clients and solicited employees while Mr. Walston was still an employee of Gill are sufficiently independent to avoid preemption. (Complaint pars., 20-24.)

Plaintiff submits a proposed order for the Court's review.

Attorney, Andrew D. Castricone of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415-875-3183) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS CLINT WALSTON and SYNERGY REPROGRAPHICS, LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, filed pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure 430.10(c) on the grounds that there is another action pending between the same parties on the same cause of action is OVERRULED. California Code of Civil Procedure 430.10(c) only applies in circumstances where another action is pending between the same parties on the same cause of action in the same state. (Leadford v. Leadford (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 571, 575.) Here, California Code of Civil Procedure 430.10(c) is inapplicable because the prior action is pending in Harris County, Texas. Where the prior and instant actions are pending in courts of different states, a party can bring an appropriate motion to stay in the instant action. The court has discretion to grant such motion after considering all appropriate factual matters outside the pleadings that are submitted by the moving party. (Id. at pp. 574-575.) The court takes judicial notice of the Complaint Walston v. Gill Reprographics, Inc., Case No. 2017-12496 ("Harris County Action"), the Counterclaim filed by Gill Reprographics, Inc. in the Harris County Action, the Declaration of Andrew D. Castricone, Esq. pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 430.41 and the Stipulated Application and [Proposed] Oder to Extend Time to Respond to Complaint. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
